post.description: Returns either a JWT access token or a short-lived MFA challenge token when the account requires a second factor.

200.description: Successful login or MFA challenge response.

400.description: Password reset token was invalid or expired.

get.description: Returns stable claim/realtime counters for the diagnostics panel.

post.description: Called by an agent before registration is approved. Generates a short-lived enrollment token, stores only hashed token material in the database, and returns the raw token plus expiration time to the caller.
post.summary: Initiate two-step agent enrollment

post.description: Admin-only endpoint used by the web app. If the token is still pending, unexpired, and matches the provided email, the API creates a node, issues a fresh agentToken, and marks the enrollment as approved.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Short-lived enrollment token originally returned to the agent.

201.description: Enrollment approved and node credentials issued.

404.description: Enrollment token was not found or email did not match.

409.description: Enrollment token has already been approved.

410.description: Enrollment token expired or was revoked.

post.summary: Finalize agent enrollment and create a node

get.description: Called by the agent while waiting for web approval. Pending tokens return only status, approved tokens return status plus nodeId and agentToken, and expired pending tokens are immediately revoked.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Short-lived enrollment token used by the agent while polling for approval.

200.description: Current enrollment status and approved credentials when available.

403.description: Workspace owner or admin access required.

post.summary: Finalize agent enrollment for a workspace

201.description: Node created and agent credentials issued.

post.summary: Consume a one-click node install token
Report one-click installer progress

403.description: Workspace owner or admin access required.

post.summary: Generate a one-click node install command for a workspace

get.summary: Get live one-click node install status for a workspace

get.description: Returns all registered nodes, optionally filtered by status or search term. Node status is maintained by heartbeats plus the background offline detector.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Free-text search against node name and hostname.

parameters.name: maintenanceMode
parameters.description: Filter nodes currently in maintenance mode.

post.description: Administrative endpoint for creating node records without agent registration.
post.summary: Create a node manually

get.description: Returns a single node with its current online or offline status and last heartbeat timestamp.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Free-text search against node name and hostname.

parameters.name: maintenanceMode
parameters.description: Filter nodes currently in maintenance mode.

post.summary: Create a node in a workspace

get.summary: Get a workspace node by ID

delete.summary: Delete a node in a workspace

get.description: Queues a packageList task for the node, waits up to 10 seconds for completion, and returns normalized package records when the agent responds in time. If the task is still queued or running after the wait window, the endpoint returns 202 so the client can continue with the existing task and log APIs.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Target node identifier.

200.description: Installed packages or a terminal task error payload.

202.description: Task created but not yet completed.

get.summary: List installed packages for a node

post.description: Queues a packageInstall task for the target node. The response returns a task identifier immediately so the frontend can follow progress via the existing task detail and task log endpoints.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Target node identifier.

requestBody.description: Package install request payload. The purge flag is forwarded to the packageInstall task payload for agent compatibility.

post.summary: Install packages on a node

get.description: Queues a packageSearch task for the target node. Debian APT documents search behavior against package names and descriptions, and related apt-style output includes package metadata plus a short description. References: https://manpages.debian.org/experimental/apt/apt.8.en.html and https://manpages.debian.org/testing/apt/apt-cache.8.en.html

parameters.name: term
parameters.description: Search term passed to the packageSearch task.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Node whose package repositories should be searched.

200.description: Structured package search results or a terminal task error payload.

202.description: Task created but not yet completed.

get.summary: Search packages available to a node

delete.description: Queues either a packageRemove or packagePurge task depending on the purge query parameter. Debian documents that remove leaves configuration files in place, while purge removes configuration files too: https://manpages.debian.org/experimental/apt/apt-get.8.en.html

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Target node identifier.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Package name to remove from the node.

parameters.name: purge
parameters.description: When true, queues packagePurge. When false or omitted, queues packageRemove.

delete.summary: Remove or purge a package on a node

get.summary: List installed packages for a workspace node

403.description: Workspace owner or admin access required.

post.summary: Install packages on a workspace node

get.summary: Search packages for a workspace node

delete.summary: Remove or purge a package on a workspace node

patch.description: Writes installer-managed runtime settings to install-state. A restart is required to apply them.

post.description: Tests SMTP connectivity for the submitted draft settings without persisting any changes.

post.description: Requests a controlled API restart. The response is flushed before the current process exits, and an external supervisor should bring the service back.

post.description: Legacy single-step registration path. Creates or refreshes a node record and returns the node ID with an agent token. Prefer the new two-step enrollment flow using /enrollments/initiate, /enrollments/:token/finalize, and /enrollments/:token.

requestBody.description: Agent identity payload plus the shared enrollment token required for initial registration.

post.summary: Register a new agent (legacy)

post.description: Authenticates the agent token, updates last seen time, and marks the node online. Nodes that stop sending heartbeats are later marked offline by the background offline detector.

401.description: Invalid node ID or agent token.

post.summary: Send an agent heartbeat

get.description: Returns a lightweight health payload for uptime and load balancer checks.
Returns dependency-aware readiness for deploys and active traffic checks.
Returns individual dependency checks for PostgreSQL, Redis, install state, and migrations.
Returns internal runtime and control-plane metrics for private scraping targets.

info.title: Noderax API
info.description: Noderax control-plane API for setup, orchestration, realtime, agent updates, and installer-managed control-plane updates.

bearer.description: Paste a JWT access token to authorize protected endpoints.

statusMessage.example: Paused after srv-02 failed checksum verification.

version.description: Requested official tagged release. When omitted, the latest official tagged release is used.

rollback.description: Marks the rollout as a rollback operation for UI and audit surfaces.

message.example: Verifying the official amd64 binary checksum.

description.example: Handles infra operations and runtime maintenance.
Handles infra operations and runtime maintenance.

accessToken.description: JWT access token for authenticated requests.

requiresMfa.description: Returned when password validation succeeded but MFA is still required.

mfaChallengeToken.description: Short-lived token used to finish MFA verification.

token.description: Current authenticator app code used to confirm enrollment.
Current authenticator code required before issuing a fresh recovery-code set.
Current authenticator code required before MFA can be disabled.

hostname.description: Unique hostname reported by the enrolling agent.

additionalInfo.description: Optional free-form metadata reported by the agent, such as os, arch, platformVersion, kernelVersion, or agentVersion.

token.description: Short-lived enrollment token returned to the agent. The raw token is never stored in the database.

agentToken.description: Fresh agent credential returned once enrollment is approved. Only the hash is stored on the node record.

statusMessage.example: Agent binary downloaded. Bootstrapping node credentials next.
Agent binary downloaded. Bootstrapping node credentials next.
Agent binary downloaded. Bootstrapping node credentials next.

EventSeverity.description: Which node-scoped event severities may be delivered by email when the workspace email channel is enabled.

status.description: Current node status. Background offline detection marks stale nodes offline after the configured heartbeat timeout.

teamId.description: Operational owner team for the node.

notificationEmailEnabled.description: Whether node-scoped event notifications may be delivered by email when the workspace email channel is enabled.

notificationEmailLevels.description: Which node-scoped event severities may be delivered by email when the workspace email channel is enabled.

notificationTelegramEnabled.description: Whether node-scoped event notifications may be delivered by Telegram when the workspace Telegram channel is enabled.

notificationTelegramLevels.description: Which node-scoped event severities may be delivered by Telegram when the workspace Telegram channel is enabled.

lastSeenAt.description: Timestamp of the last successful heartbeat or agent activity seen by the control plane.

name.description: Optional display name. Defaults to hostname when omitted.

teamId.description: Assign the node to a workspace team, or omit to clear.

notificationEmailEnabled.description: Allow or suppress email delivery for node-scoped events when the workspace email channel is enabled.

notificationEmailLevels.description: Allowed email severities for node-scoped events. Empty means no node-scoped emails are sent even when the email channel is enabled.

notificationTelegramEnabled.description: Allow or suppress Telegram delivery for node-scoped events when the workspace Telegram channel is enabled.

notificationTelegramLevels.description: Allowed Telegram severities for node-scoped events. Empty means no node-scoped Telegram messages are sent even when the Telegram channel is enabled.

purge.description: Forwarded to the agent for compatibility with the packageInstall task payload.

error.example: Task completed without a structured package result.
Task completed without a structured package result.

message.example: Changes are written to installer state and will apply after the API restarts.
API restart requested. The current process will exit and should come back if it is supervised by Docker, systemd, or another process manager.
Failed password for invalid user deploy from 10.0.0.12 port 53412 ssh2

agentVersion.description: Optional runtime agent version reported alongside the metrics snapshot.

collectedAt.description: Optional collection timestamp emitted by the agent alongside the metrics snapshot.

cpu.description: Go-agent compatible CPU payload. usagePercent is mapped to cpuUsage.

memory.description: Go-agent compatible memory payload. usedPercent is mapped to memoryUsage.

disk.description: Go-agent compatible disk payload. usedPercent is mapped to diskUsage.

networks.description: Go-agent compatible per-interface metrics. They are summarized into networkStats.

isInternal.description: Internal tasks are used by platform subsystems and are hidden from default operator task listings.

outputTruncated.description: True when task output was truncated to satisfy API limits.

leaseUntil.description: Lease expiry used by polling agents to avoid duplicate claims.

claimedBy.description: Node ID that currently holds the lease for this task.

claimToken.description: Opaque claim token for optimistic ownership checks.

cancelRequestedAt.description: Operator requested cancellation timestamp. Running agents should stop gracefully when this is set.

maxTasks.description: Current API supports claiming a single task per call.

outputTruncated.description: Whether output returned by completion APIs was truncated.

taskId.description: Must match route taskId when provided.

cancelRequested.description: When true, running agent should terminate current execution and report cancelled completion.

taskId.description: Must match route taskId when provided.
Must match route taskId when provided.
Must match route taskId when provided.

terminalConnectToken.description: Short-lived websocket capability token scoped to this terminal session.

terminalConnectExpiresAt.description: Expiry timestamp for the terminal websocket token.

terminalConnectToken.description: Short-lived websocket capability token scoped to this terminal session.

terminalConnectExpiresAt.description: Expiry timestamp for the terminal websocket token.

hostname.description: Unique hostname of the registering server.

os.description: Operating system label reported by the agent.

arch.description: CPU architecture reported by the agent.

enrollmentToken.description: Shared enrollment token used during initial agent registration to prevent unauthorized servers from enrolling.

agentToken.description: Agent credential returned at registration time.

agentVersion.description: Optional agent version reported with the heartbeat for observability.

platformVersion.description: Optional host platform version reported with the heartbeat for node observability.

kernelVersion.description: Optional host kernel version reported with the heartbeat for node observability.

sentAt.description: Optional timestamp generated by the agent when the heartbeat was sent.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the health response was generated.

startedAt.description: Timestamp when the current API process booted.

bootId.description: Unique identifier for the current API process instance.
